The PR changes the two `tryMatchModel()` candidate sort comparators in `src/agents/sessions/model-resolver.ts` to use numeric-aware `localeCompare`.

PR surface: Source 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked issue gives a high-confidence source path through `parseModelPattern("opus", [claude-opus-4-9, claude-opus-4-10])`, where current main chooses the older lexicographic winner.

target.title: Fix #96588: Use numeric collation for model alias version sorting
target.body:
## Summary

Add `{ numeric: true }` to `localeCompare` calls in `tryMatchModel()` so model IDs with double-digit minor versions sort correctly.

## Problem

When a model alias like `opus` matches multiple versioned model IDs (e.g. `claude-opus-4-9` and `claude-opus-4-10`), `localeCompare` without `{ numeric: true }` uses lexicographic ordering. This means `4-9` sorts above `4-10`, and the resolver silently selects the older version.

## Fix

Changed both `localeCompare` calls in `src/agents/sessions/model-resolver.ts` from:
```ts
aliases.sort((a, b) => b.id.localeCompare(a.id));
```
to:
```ts
aliases.sort((a, b) => b.id.localeCompare(a.id, undefined, { numeric: true }));
```

## Impact

This is a forward-looking fix. No shipped model family currently has double-digit minor versions, but the fix prevents incorrect alias resolution when they do.

Fixes #96588
EOF
target.author: krissding
